mission.title: flux: Create architecture telemetry in the gotk
mission.description: Create architecture telemetry in the gotk. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current flux deployment
steps.description:
Verify your flux version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
helm list -n flux 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working flux installation.

steps.title: Review flux configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flux configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
kubectl get configmap -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flux
```
Evening,

I'm inquiring for the feasibility of adding telemetry to your images hosted on ghcr. I came across this [discussion](https://github.com/fluxcd/flux2/discussions/303) earlier today.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Create architecture telemetry in the gotk
steps.description:
Happy to announce that we simply had to wait a tiny bit till GitHub released statistics per image layer, which they just did.

This means that we are now able to merge the images together and remove the arch selector from the manifests, but this will likely not happen before the new year.

Best wishes, and see you in 2021

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
kubectl get events -n flux --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Create architecture telemetry in the gotk" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Happy to announce that we simply had to wait a tiny bit till GitHub released statistics per image layer, which they just did.

This means that we are now able to merge the images together and remove the arch selector from the manifests, but this will likely not happen before the new year.

Best wishes, and see you in 2021

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with flux installed or the issue environment reproducible.
